text:
A gentle breeze rustled the leaves of the ancient oak tree as it swayed with each light touch of a feather drifted by. Its gnarled branches reached out, forming a hollow heart shaped by time and weather. In the heart of this mighty oak stood Lily, a young girl with soft eyes that sparkled like stars in a clear night sky.

Lily would often sit beneath the tree, listening to its whispers and feeling its ancient presence. It sang stories of a life once lived by the tree, tales of storms and gentle rains, of love and loss, of growth and perseverance.

One day, an old man appeared beside the tree with a worn cloak and a thoughtful gaze. He noticed Lily sitting beneath it, her hair shimmering in the sunlight like a cascade of silk. "What brings you here, young lady?" he asked.

Lily looked up, surprised. "Every day I come here to listen to your song," she replied with a smile. The old man chuckled softly
